# Brainstorm v2 — {{topic}} — Questionnaire
# Generated by: sdk/brainstorm_predictor.predict_all() + render pass
# Source protocol: protocols/brainstorm-pattern-v2.md
# Scratchpad location: _brainstorm/{{topic}}-v2-questionnaire.md
#
# PLACEHOLDER VARIABLES:
#   {{topic}}                      — brainstorm topic string
#   {{generated_at}}               — ISO timestamp of generation
#   {{context_retro_count}}        — number of relevant retros loaded
#   {{context_pattern_count}}      — number of patterns loaded
#   {{questions}}                  — list of Question + Prediction pairs
#   Per question:
#     {{q.id}}                     — question ID (e.g. "Q1")
#     {{q.text}}                   — question text
#     {{p.recommended_value}}      — AI recommended answer
#     {{p.confidence_band}}        — "high" | "medium" | "low"
#     {{p.confidence_score}}       — 0.0–1.0 float
#     {{p.rationale}}              — evidence citation string
#     {{p.alternative_options}}    — list of other options
#     {{p.evidence_sources}}       — list of block IDs / pattern names
#     {{q.options}}                — all options (including recommended)
#     {{q.allow_free_text}}        — always True (Q15)
#
# HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE:
#   1. Replace all {{placeholders}} with actual values from PredictionSet.
#   2. Save as _brainstorm/<topic>-v2-questionnaire.md.
#   3. Share with user for response.
#   4. User edits this file: marks chosen option, writes free text where needed.
#   5. Pass edited file to brainstorm_synthesis.py for design doc generation.

---

# Brainstorm v2 — {{topic}}

**Generated:** {{generated_at}}
**Context loaded:** {{context_retro_count}} retrospectives · {{context_pattern_count}} patterns

> **How to respond:**
> For each question, either:
> - Mark the AI-recommended option with `[x]` to accept it, or
> - Mark a different option with `[x]`, or
> - Write your own answer in the "Other" field (always allowed).
> You can also add notes after your selection.

---

## Questions

<!-- Repeat this block for each question in the questionnaire -->

### {{q.id}}. {{q.text}}

<!-- Confidence indicators: 🟢 high | 🟡 medium | 🔴 low -->
🤖 **AI recommends:** {{p.recommended_value}}
📊 **Confidence:** {{confidence_emoji}} {{p.confidence_band | upper}}
💡 **Rationale:** {{p.rationale}}
🔗 **Evidence:** {{p.evidence_sources | join(", ")}}

<!-- Low confidence → always show this warning -->
<!-- {{if p.is_guessing}} ⚠️ AI is guessing — please provide your own answer. {{endif}} -->

**Options:**

<!-- Options list. Recommended option shown first with ← AI pick marker. -->
- [ ] {{p.recommended_value}} ← AI pick
<!-- For each alternative option: -->
- [ ] {{alternative_option}}
<!-- Open-answer field: always present per Q15 principle -->
- [ ] Other (free text): _______________

<!-- User's response goes here: -->
**Your answer:** <!-- Mark option above OR fill "Other" field -->

**Notes (optional):** _______________

<!-- EXAMPLE rendering of one real question (for reference): -->

## Example

### Q1. How should inter-module dependencies be tracked?

🤖 **AI recommends:** Manifest `dependencies:` field + automated resolver
📊 **Confidence:** 🟢 HIGH
💡 **Rationale:** Strong evidence in context (block-107, protocols/dependency-resolution.md) supports manifest-based tracking with automated resolution.
🔗 **Evidence:** block-107, ADR-001

**Options:**

- [ ] Manifest `dependencies:` field + automated resolver ← AI pick
- [ ] Manual board.md updates only
- [ ] External task tracker
- [ ] Other (free text): _______________

**Your answer:**

**Notes (optional):**
